pi_items.source: Verified through all scripts (seed_demodb.py, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, task_utils.sh). Database name is consistently 'demodb' throughout the environment.

Task description (task.json lines 5-4): task explicitly requires creating these three new classes. StaySession is a new Vertex class, HasSession and SessionAt are new Edge classes not present in the original DemoDB schema created by seed_demodb.py.

Verified through seed_demodb.py (line 87: 'CREATE CLASS HasStayed EXTENDS E'). This edge class connects Profiles to Hotels and is created during database seeding. The task requires migrating these relationships through the new StaySession vertex and then deleting the original HasStayed edges.

setup_task.sh (lines 42-44) ensures at least 50 HasStayed edges by seeding random Profile-Hotel connections. seed_demodb.py creates 11 initial edges (lines 380-399), and setup_task.sh adds additional edges as needed to reach target of 50.

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetically generated by seed_demodb.py, using real-world references for countries, hotels, restaurants, and profiles
pi_items.source: seed_demodb.py header comment: 'Data uses real country names, real hotel names, real restaurant names, and realistic profiles.' Examples: Hotel Artemide (Rome), Hotel Adlon Kempinski (Berlin), The Savoy (London), etc. are real hotels with actual geographic coordinates and contact information.

Consistent across all environment scripts: install_orientdb.sh (line 84), setup_orientdb.sh (line 24, 91), seed_demodb.py (line 13), task_utils.sh (line 4), setup_task.sh (line 25), export_result.sh (line 15).

pi_items.verified_value: Property graph model with Vertex and Edge classes, following OrientDB's official DemoDB schema for a travel agency application
pi_items.source: seed_demodb.py docstring and implementation. DemoDB includes Profiles (users), Hotels, Restaurants, Countries, and relationships via edges like HasFriend, HasStayed, HasEaten, etc.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on OrientDB 3.2.36 database called 'demodb' containing synthetically-generated travel agency data (using real hotel/restaurant names and geographic data). The database initially contains a HasStayed edge class connecting Profile vertices to Hotel vertices with approximately 50 edges. The task requires refactoring this by creating three new classes (StaySession vertex, HasSession and SessionAt edges) to promote the relationship to first-class status, migrating all HasStayed edges through the new intermediate StaySession vertices, and then deleting the deprecated HasStayed edges. Authentication uses root / GymAnything123!
